title: Driven to Distraction
subtitle: Recognizing and Coping with Attention Deficit Disorder from Childhood Through Adulthood
author: Edward M. Hallowell and John J. Ratey
themes: ADD/ADHD is a real neurological condition, not a character flaw or lack of willpower
The core symptoms: inattention, impulsivity, and hyperactivity — but the presentation varies widely
ADD often goes undiagnosed in adults, especially inattentive type
Treatment combines medication, coaching, environmental modifications, and understanding
The positive side of ADD — creativity, energy, hyperfocus, entrepreneurial drive
ADD runs in families and is highly heritable
excerpt: ADD is not a disorder of attention. It is a disorder of attention consistency. People with ADD can pay attention — they just cannot regulate when they pay attention, to what they pay attention, or for how long. When something is interesting, they can hyperfocus for hours. When something is boring, they cannot focus at all. This inconsistency is the core of the ADD experience.
